prompt.content: As SingleSignal has rapidly grown, the team is concerned that salary bands within the same job title have spread too wide, however adjusting would come at a significant hit to culture. To get an idea of the magnitude of potential savings generated by adjusting salaries in positions with high salary spread, first identify the job titles that have a delta between the highest earner and the average earner that is above 10%. Out of those job titles, adjust any employee salary that's above the average to the average, leaving the rest unmodified, and calculate the total potential salary savings percentage if this adjustment is executed. Review the "Raw Employee Roster Export - Enhanced.xlsx" file in the filesystem, and return the answer the total potential salary savings percentage via console message rounded to one decimal place.

rubrics.name: States that the potential savings on the total payroll by lowering salaries of... (ver_3b1cebbc4234453db02bc71c98269bf6)

messages.content: States that the potential savings on the total payroll by lowering salaries of employees earning above the average, to the average in roles in which the highest earner is >10% above the average is 1.9% (acceptable range 1.88% to 1.92%).

golden_responses.golden_text_response: Lowering salaries of those employees earning above the average to the average in roles in which the highest earner is >10% above the average would result in a total potential savings of 1.9% of the total payroll.
